stem: Two chords of a circle intersect at a point \( P \) inside the circle. One chord is divided by \( P \) into segments of lengths \( [[a]] \) and \( [[b]] \). The other chord is divided by \( P \) into a segment of length \( [[c]] \) and a segment of unknown length \( y \). What is \( y \)?
solution: By the power of a point (intersecting chords), the products of the two segments of each chord are equal: \( [[a]]\cdot[[b]] = [[c]]\cdot y \). Solving, \( y = \frac{[[a]]\cdot[[b]]}{[[c]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by \( [[c]] \); the product \( [[a]]\cdot[[b]] \) must be split by the known segment of the other chord.
This treats the segments additively instead of using equal products.
This pairs the segments incorrectly, dividing by \( [[b]] \) instead of \( [[c]] \).
This pairs the segments incorrectly, dividing by \( [[a]] \).
This is off from the correct length by one.
This is off from the correct length by one.

stem: From a point \( P \) outside a circle, two secant segments are drawn. The first secant has external segment \( [[e1]] \) and total (external-to-far-intersection) length \( [[w1]] \). The second secant has external segment \( [[e2]] \) and unknown total length \( L \). What is \( L \)?
solution: By the power of a point (two secants from an external point), the product of each secant's external segment and its total length is the same: \( [[e1]]\cdot[[w1]] = [[e2]]\cdot L \). Solving, \( L = \dfrac{[[e1]]\cdot[[w1]]}{[[e2]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by the second secant's external segment.
This swaps which external segment divides which product.
This forgets to multiply by the first secant's external segment at all.
This treats the segments additively instead of using equal products.
This is off from the correct length by one.
This is off from the correct length by one.

stem: From a point \( P \) outside a circle, a tangent segment of length \( [[t]] \) and a secant are drawn. The secant's external segment (from \( P \) to the near intersection) has length \( [[ext]] \). What is the secant's total length, from \( P \) to the far intersection?
solution: By the power of a point (tangent-secant), the tangent squared equals the product of the secant's external segment and its total length: \( [[t]]^{2} = [[ext]]\cdot L \). Solving, \( L = \dfrac{[[t]]^{2}}{[[ext]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by the external segment.
This treats the relationship as a simple product of tangent and external segment rather than tangent squared.
This inverts the relationship, dividing the tangent by the external segment instead.
This swaps the roles of the tangent and the external segment.
This is off from the correct length by one.
This is off from the correct length by one.

stem: A tangent segment from an external point \( P \) to a circle of radius \( [[r]] \) has length \( [[t]] \). What is the distance from \( P \) to the center of the circle?
solution: A radius drawn to the point of tangency is perpendicular to the tangent line, so the radius, the tangent segment, and the segment from \( P \) to the center form a right triangle with the center-to-\(P\) distance as the hypotenuse. By the Pythagorean theorem, that distance is \( \sqrt{[[r]]^{2} + [[t]]^{2}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This just adds the radius and tangent length instead of combining them under a square root.
This puts the radius and tangent under the root with the wrong sign, as if the tangent were the hypotenuse.
This forgets the square root, giving the sum of the squares.
This has the radius and tangent swapped under a subtraction, again treating one of them as a hypotenuse.
This is off from the correct distance by one.
This is off from the correct distance by one.

stem: A chord of a circle with radius \( [[r]] \) lies at a perpendicular distance of \( [[d]] \) from the center. What is the length of the chord?
solution: The perpendicular from the center to a chord bisects the chord, forming a right triangle with the radius as the hypotenuse, the perpendicular distance as one leg, and half the chord as the other leg. So half the chord is \( \sqrt{[[r]]^{2} - [[d]]^{2}} \), and the full chord is \( 2\sqrt{[[r]]^{2} - [[d]]^{2}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This gives only half the chord, forgetting to double it.
This adds the squares instead of subtracting, as if the perpendicular were outside the circle.
This forgets the square root, giving the difference of squares directly.
This treats the radius and distance as sides of a simple linear difference rather than a right triangle.
This is off from the correct length by one.
This is off from the correct length by one.
